behavior_summary: This script implements the NPC/item Pyros's inspect (U8P_PYROS::look) text and a large interactive 'U8P_PYROS::use' sequence that animates Pyros, plays SFX, spawns fire/spell effects, and provokes acolytes/guards. The 'U8P_PYROS::look' process shows the short title 'Pyros, Lord of Flame'.

U8P_PYROS::use is the primary gameplay interaction: it sequences timed delays (spawn -> FREE::func1C19), runs smaller animation helpers to step visual frames (Item::getFrame/Item::setFrame via many helpers), issues player-facing barks, and ultimately spawns FIRESPEL logic and projectile/effect processes (createSprite, Item::hurl).

Transformation helpers (U8P_PYROS::pyrosTransformForward, U8P_PYROS::pyrosTransformReverse, U8P_PYROS::func16FD, U8P_PYROS::func178F, U8P_PYROS::func1EFA, U8P_PYROS::func1883) implement frame-based animations with waits and SFX. U8P_PYROS::pyrosBarkAndTransform centralizes the pattern of calling a pre-transform helper, emitting a bark, and calling a post-transform helper.

U8P_PYROS::func018D implements an effect/explosion path that computes coordinates, creates sprites, plays SFX, spawns directional helper processes, and may create and hurl a projectile — emits distressed barks tied to internal id/case.

U8P_PYROS::func247C is a dialogue/menu scene routine that builds Ask menus, captures player choices (SuspendAssign), puts the avatar in stasis, repositions NPCs/camera, performs placement checks (Item::legal_create), and orchestrates a cinematic sequence including spawn of FIRESPEL effects.
notable_control_flow: Most animation/transform steps U8P_PYROS::use the pattern: read Item::getFrame -> choose next frame or branch -> spawn FREE::func1C19 (timer) -> suspend -> Item::setFrame. This yields visibly-timed animation.
U8P_PYROS::use gates its main sequence on the NPC map (checks Npc::getMap) and branches on scene state like acolyteKilled to change spawned behavior and timing values.
Dialogue/menu flow in U8P_PYROS::func247C loops using Ask + SuspendAssign + strcmp branching on the captured choice, with setAvatarInStasis used to freeze/unfreeze the player during cinematic steps.

Explosive/effect behavior in U8P_PYROS::func018D includes a special-case for a specific id (this == 0x1B) where random offsets + Item::legal_create are used before spawning an Item::hurl — i.e., effects may attempt placement and fallback if illegal.
open_questions: Are engine references like 0x0200 and 0x020A script-local runtime handles or global engine flags that affect other scripts? (calls to FREE::resetRef are evident but their cross-script semantics remain inferred).
Which in-game triggers call U8P_PYROS::use in normal gameplay (player 'Use' vs. scripted召ons)? The script implies player activation but exact event wiring is outside this file.

Exact mapping from numeric frame values (e.g., 0x1B, 0x5F, 0x62, 0x66, 0x69) to visual states/models is unknown; understanding those would clarify when forward vs reverse transforms trigger special effects.

Are flags such as acolyteKilled, inDaemonScene, malchirMad and stormsActive global script flags managed elsewhere or local to a larger encounter manager? The script reads/writes them but their full lifecycle is external.

main_function.name: U8P_PYROS::look
main_function.purpose: Display Pyros's inspect/description text when the player examines the NPC (emit the short bark 'Pyros, Lord of Flame' then suspend).
entry_conditions: Player performs a 'U8P_PYROS::look' (inspect) action on the Pyros NPC
Engine invokes the object's U8P_PYROS::look/inspect handler (process type 0x0208)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::use
function_map.role: primary interaction/activation process that sequences transforms, barks, and fire-spell effects

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::pyrosBarkAndTransform
function_map.role: Emit a bark (text param) and run pre/post transform helpers to animate around the line

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::pyrosTransformForward
function_map.role: Advance visual frame state with timed pauses (forward transform)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::pyrosTransformReverse
function_map.role: Reverse visual frame state with timed pauses (reverse transform)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func16FD
function_map.role: Choose a randomized next frame and apply it after a spawned timer (pre-bark animation step)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func178F
function_map.role: Normalize/adjust frame values and reset a runtime reference (post-bark frame normalization)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func018D
function_map.role: Create explosion/projectile sprite, spawn directional effects, play SFX, and emit distressed barks (effectful attack sequence)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func1A03
function_map.role: High-level effect/spell staging (spawn FIRESPEL, move NPCs, set avatar stasis, guardian/acolyte barks, apply damage) used during dramatic sequence

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func247C
function_map.role: Dialogue/menu scene handler: builds Ask menus, captures player choices, stages cinematic placement and spawns fire effects

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func1EFA
function_map.role: Small SFX + frame stepper used inside larger sequences

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func1883
function_map.role: Auxiliary frame/animation step used by U8P_PYROS::use sequence (timed step, low-level animation)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func231E
function_map.role: Trigger hostile bark/sequence that clears avatar stasis and sets combat target/state (inDaemonScene -> cleared, malchirMad set)

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::func3E82
function_map.role: Short scripted interaction building menus and handling choice-driven animations/barks

internal_calls.purpose: pre-bark frame/timer preparation (pick next frame and schedule a delay before setting it)
post-bark frame normalization/reset of runtime reference after bark
Emit key spoken lines while animating Pyros between transforms
Advance Pyros's frame state during the commanded transformation sequence
Restore Pyros's frame state after transformation steps
Stage the main explosion/fire-spell effect during the U8P_PYROS::use sequence (teleport NPCs, spawn FIRESPEL, set stasis, guardian barks)
Spawn directional helper effects (method calls) tied to NPC facing during the explosion sequence
Use the same bark+transform pattern when staging parts of the dialogue/cinematic scene

external_calls.purpose: Read current visual frame to choose next randomized frame
Pick a randomized next frame value from a range depending on current frame
Schedule a short timer/delay between frame changes
Create a visual explosion/sprite at computed offsets from Pyros
Check whether placing a projectile/item at computed coordinates is allowed before creating/hurling it
Launch a created projectile with randomized velocities as part of the effect
Spawn/trigger the fire-spell effect tied to Pyros's commanded power
Freeze the avatar during the cinematic/effect sequence
Apply damage/hit to an item/NPC as part of the effect sequence
Verify placement for temporary scene objects (candles/pentacle) before creating them
Set NPC combat/AI target after the hostile bark sequence
Check and clear avatar stasis so the player can act after the sequence
Play a specific sound effect and adjust its volume for a notable animation step
Reset engine runtime reference/flag (cleanup engine state after the event)

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Affects branching in U8P_PYROS::use and U8P_PYROS::func247C to change timing/behavior (influence whether acolytes react or a different effect path is used)

reads.name: rainStormActive
reads.likely_effect: Used in U8P_PYROS::func247C to choose whether to run fade-to-palette cinematic steps during the scene

writes.name: stormsActive
writes.likely_effect: Cleared to 0 during scene setup/placement in U8P_PYROS::func247C (affects global storm/visual state)

writes.name: inDaemonScene
writes.likely_effect: Cleared to 0 in U8P_PYROS::func231E to mark exit from a daemon/scene mode

writes.name: malchirMad
writes.likely_effect: Set to 3 in U8P_PYROS::func231E to mark the NPC/encounter as enraged/mad, likely switching AI to hostile behavior

writes.name: engine runtime refs (e.g., 0x0200, 0x020A, 0x00F0)
writes.likely_effect: Written/cleared via FREE::resetRef / Kernel::resetRef to manage engine-level handles/state during/after transforms and effects; affects cross-script/runtime interactions

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the NPC/item Pyros's inspect (U8P_PYROS::look) text and a large interactive 'U8P_PYROS::use' sequence that animates Pyros, plays SFX, spawns fire/spell effects, and provokes acolytes/guards. The 'U8P_PYROS::look' process shows the short title 'Pyros, Lord of Flame'.
